message:
fix(send_message): replace silent exception handlers with logging

The send_message function had 14 bare except-pass blocks that silently
swallowed errors, making lock wedges and delivery failures invisible
on Windows and other platforms. Replace each with appropriate logging:

- logger.exception() for operational failures (DB queries, handshakes,
  delivery, registration) to surface the actual root cause
- logger.debug(exc_info=True) for expected parse/resolution failures
  in address routing (project:slug#name, name@project formats)
- Narrow int(thread_id) catch to (ValueError, TypeError) since only
  numeric parse failures are expected there

This provides observability into what was previously a diagnostic
black hole, particularly for the Windows lock wedging reported in #91.

Closes #91

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
